contract.name: v2/references/glossary.md
contract.description: Returns USAspending glossary entries (terms + definitions/resources) used to power glossary components; supports pagination via query parameters.

page.constraints: Optional. Accepts integer strings (including leading zeros). Non-integer strings (e.g., '1.5' or '') return 400 with JSON {detail}. Values < 1 return 422 with JSON {detail}. Pages beyond the available range return 200 with empty results.

limit.description: Maximum number of glossary entries per page.
limit.constraints: Optional. Accepts integer strings (including leading zeros). Non-integer strings (e.g., '1.0' or '') return 400 with JSON {detail}. Values outside 1..500 return 422 with JSON {detail}. When omitted, the API currently returns all entries on page 1 (page_metadata.count=149 at last verification), so clients should set limit explicitly to control payload size.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata. count is the total number of glossary entries; next/previous are page numbers or null. Note: if you request page > last_page+1, the API sets previous=null and hasPrevious=false even though earlier pages exist.

next.description: Next page number when available; otherwise null.

previous.description: Previous page number when available; otherwise null.

hasNext.description: True when a next page exists.

hasPrevious.description: True when a previous page exists.

results.description: Glossary entries for the requested page (may be empty for out-of-range pages).

data_act_term.description: DATA Act term text when available; null for some entries.

official.description: Official definition text when available; null for some entries.

quirks: Use the trailing-slash path: requesting '/api/v2/references/glossary' returns a 301 redirect with an empty text/html body.
Type validation errors return 400; range validation errors return 422; both return JSON objects shaped like {"detail": "..."}.
Observed constraints: page >= 1; limit in [1, 500] inclusive.
Omitting limit currently returns all entries on page 1 (page_metadata.count=149 at last verification); requesting page > 1 with omitted limit returns empty results.
Requesting pages beyond the last page returns 200 with results: [] (no 4xx).
For page > last_page+1, page_metadata.previous becomes null and hasPrevious=false even though earlier pages exist.
Unknown query parameters are ignored (no validation error).
When query parameters repeat (e.g., page=1&page=2), the last value wins.
Glossary entry fields data_act_term, official, and resources are nullable in practice; keys are still present.
risks: Clients should treat data_act_term and official as nullable despite docs (and resources may be null as documented).
Clients should always set limit explicitly to avoid unexpectedly large payloads if the glossary grows.
Clients should use the trailing-slash URL; otherwise some HTTP clients will see a 301 + empty body (text/html).
Do not rely on hasPrevious/previous when accidentally requesting pages far beyond the end; page > last_page+1 yields hasPrevious=false even though earlier pages exist.
Do not rely on the server to reject typoed query parameters; unknown query params are ignored.
gaps: Default numeric limit value is not observable from probes because omitting limit returns all results on one page (count=149 at last verification).
Result ordering is undocumented; ordering was stable across repeated calls during pass-2 but may change across deployments.

probes.notes: baseline shape + nullability audit: page_metadata+results only; results_len=149; keys never missing=True; null_counts={'data_act_term': 62, 'official': 53, 'resources': 87}.
pagination beyond last page: returns 200 + empty results; previous/hasPrevious remain set only through page=last_page+1.
pagination far beyond last page: previous becomes null and hasPrevious=false.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'page'. '1.5' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: missing trailing slash returns 301 redirect to '/api/v2/references/glossary/'.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"page_metadata":{"page":1,"count":149,"next":2,"previous":null,"hasNext":true,"hasPrevious":false},"results":[{"term":"Account Balance (File A)","slug":"account-balance-file-a","data_act_term":"Account Balance (File A)","plain":"After the end of every month (or in some select cases every quarter), agencies report the …(len=300)","official":"Account Balance data is reported in File A, one of the three files that each agency publis…(len=738)","resources":"See also:\n\n- [Account Breakdown by Program Activity & Object Class (File B)](?glossary=acc…(len=421)"}]}

mismatches: Docs mark data_act_term as a required string, but live responses include null values.
Docs mark official as a required string, but live responses include null values.
gaps: Default numeric limit value is not observable from probes because omitting limit returns all results on one page (count=149 at last verification).
Result ordering is undocumented; ordering was stable across repeated calls during pass-2 but may change across deployments.
risks: Clients should treat data_act_term and official as nullable despite docs (and resources may be null as documented).
Clients should always set limit explicitly to avoid unexpectedly large payloads if the glossary grows.
Clients should use the trailing-slash URL; otherwise some HTTP clients will see a 301 + empty body (text/html).
Do not rely on hasPrevious/previous when accidentally requesting pages far beyond the end; page > last_page+1 yields hasPrevious=false even though earlier pages exist.
Do not rely on the server to reject typoed query parameters; unknown query params are ignored.
